author: TheSeanLavery
title: Performance and Consistency Improvements — regex, file-IO cache, AST parse cache
notes: Three real observations: (1) re.findall with string patterns inside file loops — valid but overstated since Python internally caches compiled patterns. (2) 30+ files bypass read_file_text cache during scan — confirmed, file cache is active during detector runs. (3) 12 Python detector files call ast.parse independently with no shared cache — valid, real CPU overhead. All are genuine optimization targets but the submission is a plan/proposal only with no code. Regex claim is the weakest; AST cache and file-IO bypass are the most impactful.
